2313:41.name: variant=primary, state=default, isDisabled=false
2313:41.description:
variant: primary
Solid brand-colored background with high contrast text and icons providing maximum visual prominence for primary actions and key user decisions.


state: default
Button displays in standard visual state with base variant styling applied. Text appears centered with normal opacity and contrast. Icons, when present, display with standard styling matching text treatment.


isDisabled: falseStandard interactive functionality enabled with full variant styling, complete interaction state support, and normal cursor behavior over button area.


Icon Configuration Variations
hasStartIcon: true
Icon element appears positioned before button text with appropriate spacing. Icon styling matches current variant and interactive state, participating in all hover, focus, and active state changes alongside the text.
Conditional Element Behavior:
Icon color coordinates with text color based on variant styling
Interactive states apply consistently to both icon and text elements
Spacing between icon and text maintains visual balance
hasStartIcon: false
No start icon present. Button text begins at natural starting position without additional spacing or icon considerations.
hasEndIcon: true
Icon element appears positioned after button text with appropriate spacing. Icon styling coordinates with variant and participates in interactive state changes. Common for directional actions or external link indicators.
Conditional Element Behavior:
Icon color matches text color treatment for visual consistency
Interactive states affect both text and icon simultaneously
Spacing between text and icon maintains proper visual hierarchy
hasEndIcon: false
No end icon present. Button text ends at natural completion position without trailing icon elements or additional spacing.

Conditional Element Behavior:
Icon color matches text color treatment for visual consistency
Interactive states affect both text and icon simultaneously

hasEndIcon: false
No end icon present. Button text ends at natural completion position without trailing icon elements or additional spacing.

Transparent background with text and icon-only presentation providing minimal visual impact while maintaining full button functionality and accessibility.


isDisabled: trueNon-interactive state with reduced opacity and muted styling across all variant types. Text and icons appear grayed out or faded. Click interactions disabled with cursor remaining default type.


Icon Configuration Variations
hasStartIcon: true
Icon element appears positioned before button text with appropriate spacing. Icon styling matches current variant and interactive state, participating in all hover, focus, and active state changes alongside the text.
Conditional Element Behavior:
Icon color coordinates with text color based on variant styling
Interactive states apply consistently to both icon and text elements
Spacing between icon and text maintains visual balance
hasStartIcon: false
No start icon present. Button text begins at natural starting position without additional spacing or icon considerations.
hasEndIcon: true
Icon element appears positioned after button text with appropriate spacing. Icon styling coordinates with variant and participates in interactive state changes. Common for directional actions or external link indicators.
Conditional Element Behavior:
Icon color matches text color treatment for visual consistency
Interactive states affect both text and icon simultaneously
Spacing between text and icon maintains proper visual hierarchy
hasEndIcon: false
No end icon present. Button text ends at natural completion position without trailing icon elements or additional spacing.

2313:42.name: Button
2313:42.description:
An interactive button element featuring text labels with optional decorative icons positioned at the start or end of the button. The component provides multiple visual treatments including solid backgrounds, transparent presentations, and specialized knockout effects for different interface contexts. It supports comprehensive interaction state coverage including default, hover, active, and focus states, with optional disabled functionality. The button maintains consistent proportions and accessibility standards across all styling variations while providing clear visual hierarchy through variant selection.


Property-Based Instance Descriptions
Visual Variantsprimary: Solid brand-colored background with high visual prominence for main actions
secondary: Neutral background treatment for supporting actions and standard interactions
bare: Transparent background with text-only presentation for minimal visual impact
knockout: Dark background styling for contrast contexts and light interface areas
Interactive Statesdefault: Standard display state with base styling
hover: Visual feedback when cursor is positioned over button
active: Pressed state styling during user interaction
focus: Keyboard focus indicator for accessibility navigation
Disabled StateisDisabled: true: Non-interactive state with reduced opacity and muted styling
isDisabled: false: Standard interactive functionality enabled
Icon ConfigurationhasStartIcon: true: Icon positioned before button text
hasStartIcon: false: No start icon present
hasEndIcon: true: Icon positioned after button text
hasEndIcon: false: No end icon present
startIcon: Configurable icon element for start position
endIcon: Configurable icon element for end position
Text Contenttext: Accepts custom text strings for button label content


Usage GuidelinesPrimary Use CasesPrimary actions requiring high visibility and user engagement
Form submissions, confirmations, and critical user decisions
Navigation actions between major interface sections
Call-to-action buttons in marketing or promotional contexts
Modal and dialog confirmations requiring clear action choices
Implementation ConsiderationsUse primary variant for main actions requiring maximum visibility
Apply secondary variant for supporting actions with moderate emphasis
Implement bare variant for subtle actions or text-like interactions
Use knockout variant for dark backgrounds or high contrast requirements
Include start icons for contextual actions or visual categorization
Add end icons for directional actions or external navigation indicators
Ensure button text clearly describes the action or destination
Provide adequate spacing between buttons in button groups
Accessibility RequirementsButton text must clearly communicate the intended action
All interaction states must be keyboard accessible with clear focus indicators
Icons require descriptive alt text or aria-labels when present
Disabled buttons should be announced as non-interactive to screen readers
Focus indicators must be visible and distinguishable from other interaction states
